home.blurb: A free, browser-based DAW for everyone. No paywalls. No locks. Just play.

features.play: Live Play with custom key bindings
features.chaos: Chaos Machine — XY pad, granular, glitch
features.record: Record from mic or upload your own

audio.pickInstrument: Pick an instrument to load its effect chain.

recorder.title: Recorder
recorder.noClips: No clips yet. Record or drop a file.
recorder.dropOrRecord: Drop a file here, or hit record.

tune.title: Vocal tuning
tune.subtitle: Snap pitch to a key
tune.noPitch: Couldn't lock onto a pitch — try a louder, more sustained section.

export.title: Export & share
export.audioHint: Records the master output for one bar at the current BPM. Plays back briefly while exporting.
export.midiHint: Exports the active pattern's melodic notes as a Standard MIDI File. Drum tracks are skipped.
export.shareHint: Generates a link that loads this exact pattern in any browser. Includes a downloadable QR code.

share.imported: Pattern imported! Heading to the studio.
share.failed: Couldn't read this share link. It may be malformed or out of date.

auth.notConfigured: Sign-in needs Supabase env vars set.

help.title: Keyboard shortcuts
help.dropClip: Drop an audio file to import

notFound.title: Track not found
notFound.hint: We hit a wrong note. The page you wanted isn't here.

ai.title: AI Assistant
ai.chatEmpty: Ask anything about music. Try "how do I make a sad chord progression?"
ai.describePlaceholder: e.g. trap beat at 140 bpm with snappy snares
ai.genReady: Track ready below — review and apply.
ai.genFailed: AI returned no usable data. Try again with more detail.
ai.analyzeHint: Reads the most recent notes you've played and tells you the scale, chord names, and mood.
ai.notConfigured: AI needs the ai-music Edge Function deployed. See SETUP.md.

mp.notConfigured: Multiplayer needs Supabase env vars set.

loop.title: Loop Station
loop.empty: No layers yet. Pick a source and tap record.

chaos.chaosHint: Adds random pitch jitter to anything currently playing.

beat.noClipsHint: Record or upload a clip first
beat.fromClipExplain: “From clip” turns one of your own recordings or uploaded audio files into a beat-maker track. Open the recorder drawer at the bottom to record from your mic or import a sound — then come back here and it'll be available.
beat.emptyPattern: Empty pattern. Add a track to start.

mastering.title: Mastering
mastering.subtitle: One-click polish for your final mix
mastering.howItWorks: Mastering shapes the entire master output (EQ → compressor → limiter). Try a preset, hit a few notes — switch any time.

off.name: Off
off.desc: No mastering — raw mix passes through unchanged.

classic.name: Classic
classic.desc: Subtle smile EQ and gentle compression. The everyday master.

soft.name: Soft
soft.desc: Warm low end, shaved highs, slow comp — for ballads and acoustic.

podcast.name: Podcast
podcast.desc: Vocal-shaped EQ and heavy comp to even out talking dynamics.

sub-boost.name: Sub Boost
sub-boost.desc: Big low shelf for trap, phonk, and bass-heavy genres.

punchy.name: Punchy
punchy.desc: Mid push, fast comp attack — transients pop forward.

louder.name: Louder
louder.desc: Heavy compression and brick-wall limiter. Trades detail for level.

cranked.name: Cranked
cranked.desc: Scooped mids, lifted highs, hard squeeze — EDM master.

cassette.name: Cassette
cassette.desc: Tilt EQ and tape colour. Lo-fi mid-fidelity vibe.

loops.title: Sound library
loops.footerNote: Loops play through your studio's voices, theme, and mastering preset — they sound like your project.

fxlib.title: Effects library
fxlib.footerNote: Click any effect to add it to the active instrument.

desc.reverb: Adds space — small room to vast hall.
desc.delay: Echoes the signal in time — slap-back or rhythmic.
desc.chorus: Thickens and widens with subtle pitch modulation.
desc.bitcrusher: Lo-fi digital crunch by reducing bit depth.
desc.compressor: Evens out dynamics, glues the mix together.

drums.hint: Click harder (lower on the pad) for higher velocity

guitar.stringsHint: Click and drag down through the strings to strum.
guitar.notationHint: Toggle between Do Re Mi and C D E note names
guitar.chordCardHint: Pick this chord — its strings appear below.
guitar.chordPlayHint: Drag across strings to strum, or tap any single string.

binding.title: Key Bindings
binding.clickToAssign: Click any key to assign a sound, clip, chord, or action.
binding.label: Label
binding.labelHint: Optional label shown on the key

learn.title: Learn
learn.subtitle: From zero music theory to playing your first piece — built into the studio.
learn.noTheoryNeeded: No theory needed — every example plays out loud when you tap it.

tour.title: 1 · A 60-second tour
tour.intro: Five tabs at the top of the studio cover everything you can do. Here is what each one is for, in plain words.

tabs.live: Live Play — pick an instrument (piano, guitar, bass, drums…) and tap or use your keyboard to play it. Start here.
tabs.beat: Beat Maker — a 16-step grid for making drum and bass patterns. You click squares; the cursor walks across and triggers them.
tabs.loop: Loop Station — record a short snippet, layer another on top, and stack 8 layers into a live loop.
tabs.chaos: Chaos — an XY pad and random-melody generator for sound design and happy accidents.
tabs.learn: Learn — this tab. Teaches you everything else.

tour.shortcuts: Press ? at any time to see the keyboard shortcuts. Press Ctrl+A to open the AI assistant. Everything else is buttons — you never have to type a value.

notes.title: 2 · What is a note?
notes.intro: A note is a single musical sound. Music uses 12 notes that repeat — the alphabet of sound.
notes.naming: Their names are: C, D, E, F, G, A, B — and then back to C. The five black keys on a piano sit between them and are named with sharps (♯) or flats (♭).
notes.octave: The number after the letter is the octave — how high or low. C4 is the C in the middle of a piano ("middle C"). C5 is the C one octave higher (twice the frequency, sounds the same but higher). C3 is one octave lower.
notes.tryTitle: Tap each note to hear it
notes.tryHint: C4 → D4 → E4 climbs up. C5 is the same letter as C4 but higher.
notes.octaveDemo: Compare octaves: same letter, different height.

chords.title: 3 · Reading chord names
chords.intro: A chord is several notes played at the same time. The name tells you which notes and what mood.
chords.rule1: The first letter is the root note — that is the lowest, anchoring note of the chord. C means the chord is built on C.
chords.rule2: If nothing follows the letter (just "C"), it is a major chord — bright, happy.
chords.rule3: A small "m" means minor — sadder, more pensive. So "Am" is A minor.
chords.rule4: A 7 means add the seventh — jazzier, less resolved. So "G7" is G with that extra colour.
chords.rule5: "maj7" means major-seventh — dreamy, smooth. "Cmaj7" is the dreamiest of the C chords.
chords.decode: Cmaj7 = root C + major + dreamy 7th. Am7 = root A + minor + jazzy 7th. Fmaj7, G7, Em7 follow the same rule.
chords.tryTitle: Tap each chord to hear it
chords.tryHint: Notice how Cmaj7 sounds dreamy, Am7 sounds melancholy, G7 sounds like it wants to resolve.

rhythm.title: 4 · Reading the rhythm grid
rhythm.intro: A rhythm pattern is a row of slots. Each slot is a moment in time. A filled slot ■ means "play". An empty slot · means "don't play / hold".
rhythm.bar: A typical bar is 16 slots — count them as "1 e & a 2 e & a 3 e & a 4 e & a". One bar at 80 BPM lasts about 3 seconds.
rhythm.example: Here is the pattern from earlier: ■·■·■■·■·■·■·■■·
rhythm.exampleBreakdown: Hits land on positions 1, 3, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15. The rest are silent. That is what a kick + snare groove looks like written down.
rhythm.tryHint: Each ■ becomes a click; each · is silence. The cursor moves left to right at the BPM you set.

piece.title: 5 · Putting it together: read this piece
piece.intro: Now read a real piece — chords, melody, rhythm. This is exactly the example you may have seen before.
piece.melody: C4 E4 G4 C5 A4 G4 F4 E4 D4 C4 E4 G4 A4 G4 F4 E4
piece.step1: Key & mood — five chords in C major. Cmaj7 and Am7 give a dreamy, slightly jazzy mood.
piece.step2: Tempo — start slow, around 80 BPM. Each chord lasts one bar.
piece.step3: Melody — 16 notes that walk up to C5 and back down. C4 is middle C, C5 is the next C up.
piece.step4: Rhythm — the 16-slot pattern is your strumming/hit pattern under the melody. The melody lands on each slot too.

playing.title: 6 · How to play this in the studio
playing.intro: Three ways to play the piece — pick the one that fits you.
playing.wayA: A · Tap the chords on Live Play.
playing.wayAStep1: Open Live Play, pick Piano or Guitar.
playing.wayAStep2: Tap the chord buttons in order: Cmaj7, Am7, Fmaj7, G7, Em7. Each one rings out for a moment — that is one bar.
playing.wayAStep3: Then tap the melody notes (C4, E4, G4, C5…) over the top, one at a time.
playing.wayB: B · Build it in Beat Maker.
playing.wayBStep1: Open Beat Maker. Click Add Track → Bass → root note (C2 for the first bar).
playing.wayBStep2: Click step 1 of the bass row to put a hit on beat 1. The cursor will trigger it every bar.
playing.wayBStep3: Add a Drums track for kick and snare, paint the rhythm pattern, hit Play.
playing.wayC: C · Let the AI assistant lay it out for you.
playing.wayCStep1: Press Ctrl+A to open the AI panel.
playing.wayCStep2: Go to Generate, type "slow dreamy progression in C major with melody", hit generate.
playing.wayCStep3: Click "Apply to Beat Maker" — the chords, drums, and bassline land in a new pattern.

next.title: 7 · What to explore next
next.loopstation: Record a short loop on Loop Station, then layer another instrument on top. Eight layers max.
next.chaos: Drag the Chaos XY pad while a chord rings — that is sound design.
next.multiplayer: Open the AppNav menu and create a room. Send the code to a friend; you both play live in the same studio.
next.share: In Beat Maker → Export, generate a share link. Anyone who opens it loads the exact same pattern.

about.title: About KLabMusic
about.subtitle: Music was never just sound to me. It's emotion, energy, creativity, and freedom.
about.bioRole: Computer Engineering & Software Systems · Ain Shams University
about.bioP1: I'm Khaled Elsaeed, a Computer Engineering & Software Systems graduate from Ain Shams University, combining technology with a deep passion for music and musical creativity.
about.bioP2: I respect every form of music creation — whether it comes from a professional instrument, an old guitar, random scraps, or even empty barrels turned into rhythm. Music has no limits, and neither should creativity.
about.bioP3: That idea inspired me to build KLabMusic — a platform where anyone can create, experiment, learn, and enjoy music in their own way.
about.feature5: A space to explore music without boundaries
about.personal: I personally play guitar — not professionally, but passionately — and I believe music isn't about perfection, it's about expression.
about.closing: This project was built for people who love sound, rhythm, experimentation, and creating moments through music.
about.creator: Created by Khaled Elsaeed
